ops.title: Long-doc authoring: checkout buffer or MCP body param, never scratch files
ops.body:
Two first-class paths carry a long body into a doc or note; a session scratchpad file piped to `--body -` is the anti-pattern both replace.

## CLI file mode (agents without MCP, humans)

`cc-notes doc add [TITLE] --checkout --when ... --tag ... --path ...` writes a PREFILLED frontmatter buffer under `<git-common-dir>/cc-notes/edit/` and prints its path; write the markdown below the frontmatter with normal file tools; `cc-notes doc add --apply <path> [--attach <file>]...` creates the doc born-verified, attachments in the same create transaction.

Invariants that are design decisions, not accidents:
- The buffer IS the body. An empty-body apply is rejected even with `--attach`; attach-only docs stay flag-mode (`doc add --attach` with no body). On flag-mode `doc edit`, `--body ""` is legal only alongside `--attach` (converts to attach-only), mirroring add.
- The file-mode flag allowlist is computed per branch: checkout takes metadata flags (when/tag/commit/path/dir/branch, optional TITLE); apply takes `--attach` only; edit file-mode and abort take none. `edit --apply --attach` is rejected on purpose — attach to an existing entity via flag-mode `edit --attach [--replace]`.
- Commit anchors are canonicalized on BOTH sides of the apply diff and identical add/remove pairs cancel: re-spelling a 40-char SHA as its short prefix in a buffer is a no-op, not an anchor drop.
- Attachments never enter the frontmatter codec (`ParsedDoc` has no attachments key); the mount serves them read-only under /attachments. Attach is flags-only, by contract.

## MCP body param (agents with the plugin)

The `doc_add`/`note_add`/`doc_edit`/`note_edit` tools (`mcp__plugin_cc-notes_cc-notes__*`) take the full markdown as the `body` param — no buffer round-trip, no file at all. File mode (`--checkout`/`--apply`) is deliberately absent from the tool surface; the body param supersedes it. `log_append`'s content field is `text`, and `task_add` requires `criteria` (or `no_validation_criteria: true`).

## Nudge contract

Hook nudges render MCP tool wording only when `mcp_active()` holds — a capt-hook session flag flipped by observed cc-notes MCP tool calls, else a live-pid marker at `<git-common-dir>/cc-notes/mcp/<pid>.json`. When the server is absent the CLI wording holds byte-for-byte, and detection never appears in a firing condition — a wrong answer may only mis-word a hint. SessionStart hooks stay CLI-neutral: MCP servers connect after they fire.
ops.when: authoring or revising long docs/notes, or changing the checkout/apply/prefill flow, the MCP write tools, or their guidance
